adapter_contract: adapter exposes backend kind, key id, and key material digest
adapter seals canonical private-state bytes with associated envelope data
adapter opens only when key policy and checkpoint metadata agree
adapter returns explicit unavailable-key errors without exposing plaintext
invariants: checkpoint semantics do not depend on a cloud enclave
sealed payload bytes are never accepted as JSON authority
backend substitution must preserve key id, algorithm, and content-hash checks
non_claims: does not implement production hardware isolation
does not make cloud confidential computing mandatory
does not implement key rotation or migration continuity
